columns.name: TravelDistanceMin
columns.description: How far do you typically travel to go fishing? (smallest selected option). Values: 0: <20km; 1: 20-50km; 2: 50-100km; 3: 100-200km; 4: >200km

columns.name: TravelDistanceMax
columns.description: How far do you typically travel to go fishing? (largest selected option). Values: 0: <20km; 1: 20-50km; 2: 50-100km; 3: 100-200km; 4: >200km

columns.name: NShortTrips
columns.description: Over the past five years, how many short fishing trips did you go on each year? Values: 0: <5 trips; 1: 6-19 trips; 2: >=20 trips

columns.name: NLongTrips
columns.description: Over the past five years, how many long fishing trips did you go on each year? Values: 0: <1 trip; 1: 2-7 trips; 2: >=8 trips

columns.name: ReportRateTripsAA

columns.name: sampleCode
columns.description: Name of the LC-MS data file

columns.name: speciesCode
columns.description: Six-character species code or morpho-species code

columns.name: Plot
columns.description: Madidi Project forest plot

columns.name: Species_binomial
columns.description: Species Latin binomial

columns.name: Genus
columns.description: Botanical genus

columns.name: Family
columns.description: Botanical family

columns.name: Abundance
columns.description: Number of individuals in the forest plot

columns.name: nAtomP
columns.description: The number of atoms in the largest aromatic, conjugated pi system, which is high in pigments and light-absorbing molecules that function to protect plants from ultraviolet radiation.

columns.name: ALogP
columns.description: Hydrophobicity measured as the log-ratio of solubility in octanol versus water; may be related to a plant-defense spectrum defined by unsaturated, aromatic, and nonpolar metabolites versus saturated and polar metabolites; negatively correlated with polarity and hence desiccation resistance.

columns.name: TopoPSA
columns.description: Topological polar surface area, the sum of the surface area of polar atoms in Ångstroms (Å), which may contribute to desiccation resistance, but is negatively correlated with passive transport through cell membranes.

columns.name: Fsp3
columns.description: The fraction of carbon atoms with sp3  electron orbits (i.e. only single bonds) to the total number of carbon atoms in the molecule, which is positively correlated with melting point, solubility, and the likelihood of a compound to exhibit bioactivity in pharmaceutical assays.

columns.name: MW
columns.description: Molecular weight in Daltons (Da); greatest in peptides and lignans, may be related to leaf longevity because peptides and lignans function as long-lived physical and storage structures.

columns.name: SLA
columns.description: Specific leaf area; a leaf economics-resource capture trait associated with high photosynthetic rates, high relative growth rates, low carbon investment in lignin or tannins, and resource-rich environments. NA indicates data not available in the Madidi Project morphological traits dataset.

columns.name: LeafArea
columns.description: Leaf area; associated with a resource-acquisitive life history strategy. NA indicates data not available in the Madidi Project morphological traits dataset.

columns.name: LeafThickness
columns.description: Leaf thickness; associated with tolerance to disturbance and nutrient stress and a resource-conservative life history strategy. NA indicates data not available in the Madidi Project morphological traits dataset.

columns.name: TwigBarkThickness_Relative
columns.description: Twig bark thickness relative to stem diameter; associated with protection from attack by pathogens and herbivores. NA indicates data not available in the Madidi Project morphological traits dataset.

columns.name: TwigSpecDens
columns.description: Twig specific density; associated with low relative growth rates, high survival, and resistance to pathogens, herbivores or physical damage. NA indicates data not available in the Madidi Project morphological traits dataset.

columns.name: nAtomP_75
columns.description: Abundance-weighted CSCS chemical similarity to co-occurring tree species with respect to the upper quartile of metabolites with respect to nAtomP

columns.name: ALogP_25
columns.description: Abundance-weighted CSCS chemical similarity to co-occurring tree species with respect to the lower quartile of metabolites with respect to ALogP

columns.name: TopoPSA_25
columns.description: Abundance-weighted CSCS chemical similarity to co-occurring tree species with respect to the lower quartile of metabolites with respect to TopoPSA

columns.name: TopoPSA_75
columns.description: Abundance-weighted CSCS chemical similarity to co-occurring tree species with respect to the upper quartile of metabolites with respect to TopoPSA

columns.name: Fsp3_75
columns.description: Abundance-weighted CSCS chemical similarity to co-occurring tree species with respect to the upper quartile of metabolites with respect to Fsp3

columns.name: Elevation
columns.description: Elevation of the forest plot in which the species occurs in meters (m)

columns.name: Plot_simp
columns.description: Simplified name of the forest plot

columns.name: Plot
columns.description: Madidi Project forest plot

columns.name: Elevation
columns.description: Elevation of the forest plot in meters (m).

columns.name: Climate_PC1
columns.description: Position of the forest plot on the first principal component in a principal component analysis (PCA); greater values reflect greater temperature and precipitation.

columns.name: Climate_PC2
columns.description: Position of the forest plot on the second principal component in a principal component analysis (PCA); greater values reflect precipitation and temperature seasonality.

columns.name: invSimpson
columns.description: Species diversity represented as the inverse Simpson index.

columns.name: nAtomP
columns.description: Community-weighted mean nAtomP, the number of atoms in the largest aromatic, conjugated pi system, which is high in pigments and light-absorbing molecules that function to protect plants from ultraviolet radiation.

columns.name: ALogP
columns.description: Community-weighted mean ALogP, hydrophobicity measured as the log-ratio of solubility in octanol versus water; may be related to a plant-defense spectrum defined by unsaturated, aromatic, and nonpolar metabolites versus saturated and polar metabolites; negatively correlated with polarity and hence desiccation resistance.

columns.name: TopoPSA
columns.description: Community-weighted mean TopoPSA, topological polar surface area, the sum of the surface area of polar atoms in Ångstroms (Å), which may contribute to desiccation resistance, but is negatively correlated with passive transport through cell membranes.

columns.name: Fsp3
columns.description: Community-weighted mean Fsp3, the fraction of carbon atoms with sp3  electron orbits (i.e. only single bonds) to the total number of carbon atoms in the molecule, which is positively correlated with melting point, solubility, and the likelihood of a compound to exhibit bioactivity in pharmaceutical assays.

columns.name: MW
columns.description: Community-weighted mean molecular weight in Daltons (Da); greatest in peptides and lignans, may be related to leaf longevity because peptides and lignans function as long-lived physical and storage structures.

columns.name: SLA
columns.description: Community-weighted mean specific leaf area; a leaf economics-resource capture trait associated with high photosynthetic rates, high relative growth rates, low carbon investment in lignin or tannins, and resource-rich environments.

columns.name: LeafArea
columns.description: Community-weighted mean leaf area; associated with a resource-acquisitive life history strategy.

columns.name: LeafThickness
columns.description: Community-weighted mean leaf thickness; associated with tolerance to disturbance and nutrient stress and a resource-conservative life history strategy.

columns.name: TwigBarkThickness_Relative
columns.description: Community-weighted mean twig bark thickness relative to stem diameter; associated with protection from attack by pathogens and herbivores.

columns.name: TwigSpecDens
columns.description: Community-weighted mean twig specific density; associated with low relative growth rates, high survival, and resistance to pathogens, herbivores or physical damage.

columns.name: PhyloPCoA1
columns.description: Position of the forest plot on the first axis of a principal coordinates analysis that reflects the phylogenetic similarity among the 16 forest plots

columns.name: PhyloPCoA2
columns.description: Position of the forest plot on the second axis of a principal coordinates analysis that reflects the phylogenetic similarity among the 16 forest plots

columns.name: nAtomP_75
columns.description: Community (abundance)-weighted mean CSCS chemical similarity of species to other co-occurring tree species with respect to metabolites in the upper quartile of nAtomP

columns.name: ALogP_25
columns.description: Community (abundance)-weighted mean CSCS chemical similarity of species to other co-occurring tree species with respect to metabolites in the lower quartile of ALogP

columns.name: TopoPSA_75
columns.description: Community (abundance)-weighted mean CSCS chemical similarity of species to other co-occurring tree species with respect to metabolites in the upper quartile of TopoPSA

columns.name: TopoPSA_25
columns.description: Community (abundance)-weighted mean CSCS chemical similarity of species to other co-occurring tree species with respect to metabolites in the lower quartile of TopoPSA

columns.name: Fsp3_75
columns.description: Community (abundance)-weighted mean CSCS chemical similarity of species to other co-occurring tree species with respect to metabolites in the upper quartile of Fsp3

columns.name: id
columns.description: Metabolite feature ID number generated by Qemistree; matches tip labels in the Qemistree dendrogram.

columns.name: X.featureID
columns.description: Metabolite feature ID number generated by MZmine.

columns.name: csi_smiles
columns.description: Predicted structure generated by Sirius.

columns.name: table_number
columns.description: Dataset input number for Qemistree

columns.name: smiles
columns.description: Predicted structure generated by Sirius.

columns.name: structure_source
columns.description: Sirius module used to predict metabolite chemical structure.

columns.name: kingdom
columns.description: Classyfire chemotaxonomical classification at the kingdom level.

columns.name: superclass
columns.description: Classyfire chemotaxonomical classification at the superclass level.

columns.name: class
columns.description: Classyfire chemotaxonomical classification at the class level.

columns.name: subclass
columns.description: Classyfire chemotaxonomical classification at the subclass level.

columns.name: direct_parent
columns.description: Classyfire chemotaxonomical classification at the most-specific, direct-parent level.

columns.name: class_results
columns.description: NPClassifier chemotaxonomical classification at the class level.

columns.name: superclass_results
columns.description: NPClassifier chemotaxonomical classification at the superclass level.

columns.name: pathway_results
columns.description: NPClassifier chemotaxonomical classification at the biosynthetic pathway level.

columns.name: isglycoside
columns.description: NPClassifier indicator, whether the metabolite is a glycoside (contains a sugar moiety).

columns.name: custom
columns.description: Classification used by the authors.

columns.name: MW
columns.description: Molecular weight in Daltons (Da); greatest in peptides and lignans, may be related to leaf longevity because peptides and lignans function as long-lived physical and storage structures.

columns.name: nAtom
columns.description: Number of atoms.

columns.name: naAromAtom
columns.description: Number of aromatic atoms

columns.name: nAtomLC
columns.description: Number of atoms in the longest chain.

columns.name: nAtomP
columns.description: The number of atoms in the largest aromatic, conjugated pi system, which is high in pigments and light-absorbing molecules that function to protect plants from ultraviolet radiation.

columns.name: nB
columns.description: Number of bonds

columns.name: nAromBond
columns.description: Number of aromatic bonds

columns.name: nRotB
columns.description: Number of rotatable bonds.

columns.name: XLogP
columns.description: Octanol/water partition coefficient calculated using a modified atom-additive model summing atomic contributions and correcting for intramolecular interactions; positive indicates affinity to octanol (i.e. nonpolar, hydrophobic), negative to water (i.e. polar, hydrophilic);

columns.name: ALogP
columns.description: Octanol/water partition coefficient calculated using a modified atom-additive model summing atom type contributions based on focal atom and bond characteristics; positive indicates affinity to octanol (i.e. nonpolar, hydrophobic), negative to water (i.e. polar, hydrophilic); may be related to a plant-defense spectrum defined by unsaturated, aromatic, and nonpolar metabolites versus saturated and polar metabolites; negatively correlated with polarity and hence desiccation resistance.

columns.name: MLogP
columns.description: Octanol/water partition coefficient calculated using a simple equation dependent on the number of C atoms and number of hetero atoms; smaller indicates affinity to octanol (i.e. nonpolar, hydrophobic), larger to water (i.e. polar, hydrophilic);

columns.name: TopoPSA
columns.description: Topological polar surface area, the sum of the surface area of polar atoms in Ångstroms (Å), which may contribute to desiccation resistance, but is negatively correlated with passive transport through cell membranes.

columns.name: tpsaEfficiency
columns.description: Molecular weight-specific topological polar surface area, the sum of the surface area of polar atoms in Ångstroms  divided by the molecular weight in Daltons (Å Da^-1^), which may contribute to desiccation resistance, but is negatively correlated with passive transport through cell membranes.

columns.name: HybRatio
columns.description: Fraction of sp3 to sp2 carbon atoms; proxy for bond saturation and three-dimensional topological complexity, which is positively correlated with melting point, solubility, and the likelihood of a compound to exhibit bioactivity in pharmaceutical assays.

columns.name: Fsp3
columns.description: The fraction of carbon atoms with sp3  electron orbits (i.e. only single bonds) to the total number of carbon atoms in the molecule, which is positively correlated with melting point, solubility, and the likelihood of a compound to exhibit bioactivity in pharmaceutical assays.

columns.name: FMF
columns.description: Ratio between size of molecular framework (ring atoms plus linkers) and size of metabolite; complexity measure positively correlated with promiscuity (number of protein targets >50% inhibited) at values above 0.65.

columns.name: ECCEN
columns.description: Eccentric connectivity index, the distance-cum-adjacency topological descriptor; higher for longer chains with less branching; correlated with size and physicochemical properties such as boiling point.

columns.name: WPATH
columns.description: Wiener path number, a topological descriptor of molecular branching that can differentiate structural isomers; correlated positively with size and boiling point.

columns.name: WPOL
columns.description: Wiener polarity number, a variant of the Wiener path number calculated using vertices (C atoms) at distance 3; correlated positively with size and boiling point.

columns.name: nHBDon
columns.description: Number of hydrogen-bond donors (e.g. OH, NH, formal charge ≥ 0).

columns.name: nHBAcc
columns.description: Number of hydrogen-bond acceptors (e.g. O/N, formal charge ≤ 0, non-ether O, non-adjacent ON)

columns.name: PC1
columns.description: Position on the first principal component of a principal component analysis on metabolite chemical properties.

columns.name: PC2
columns.description: Position on the second principal component of a principal component analysis on metabolite chemical properties.

columns.name: PC3
columns.description: Position on the third principal component of a principal component analysis on metabolite chemical properties.

columns.name: PC4
columns.description: Position on the fourth principal component of a principal component analysis on metabolite chemical properties.

columns.name: PC5
columns.description: Position on the fifth principal component of a principal component analysis on metabolite chemical properties.

columns.name: animalID

columns.name: datetime
columns.description: Date and time the VeDBA is summarised for

columns.name: VeDBA_sum
columns.description: The hourly vectorial sum of dynamic body acceleration in g

columns.name: temp
columns.description: The temperature measurement in °C

columns.name: precip
columns.description: The measurement of precipitation volume in mm

columns.name: daynight
columns.description: Categorises if the hour falls within daytime ("day"; sunrise to sunset) or nighttime ("night"; sunset to sunrise)

columns.name: year
columns.description: The year of the date

columns.name: animalID
columns.description: Unique identifier for each tracked animal

columns.name: date
columns.description: The date the VeDBA is summarised for

columns.name: VeDBA_mean_daily
columns.description: The daily average of the hourly vectorial sum of dynamic body acceleration in g

columns.name: Tmax
columns.description: The daily maximum temperature in °C

columns.name: Tmean
columns.description: The daily average temperature in °C

columns.name: precip_total
columns.description: The daily total precipitation volume in mm

columns.name: julian
columns.description: Day of the Year

columns.name: year
columns.description: The year of the date

columns.name: heatwave
columns.description: Binary category for within heatwave (1) or outside of heatwave (0)

columns.name: seq_id
columns.description: Continuous identifier for each heatwave

columns.name: seq_nr
columns.description: Continuous number of days within each heatwave (per seq_id)

columns.name: seq_length
columns.description: Maximum number of days within each heatwave (per seq_id)

columns.name: SL_sum
columns.description: daily distance traveled calculated using the sum of step length (distance between consecutive GPS locations)

columns.name: NSD_max
columns.description: daily maximum net squared displacement calculated using GPS locations

columns.name: speed_mean
columns.description: daily average speed calculated using GPS locations

columns.name: animalID
columns.description: Unique identifier for each tracked animal

columns.name: seq_id
columns.description: Continuous identifier for each heatwave

columns.name: VeDBA_mean_mean
columns.description: The mean daily average of the hourly vectorial sum of dynamic body acceleration within each heatwave in g

columns.name: Tmax_mean
columns.description: The average daily maximum temperature within each heatwave in °C

columns.name: precip_total
columns.description: The total precipitation volume within each heatwave in mm

columns.name: seq_length
columns.description: Maximum number of days within each heatwave (per seq_id)

columns.name: year
columns.description: The year each heatwave was registered

columns.name: month
columns.description: The month of the year

columns.name: year
columns.description: The year of measurement

columns.name: Tmean
columns.description: The average temperature in °C

columns.name: precip
columns.description: The total precipitation in mm

columns.name: Species
columns.description: Species name according to the tetrapod phylogeny in Chen & Wiens (2020) Nat. Comm.

columns.name: Family
columns.description: Taxonomic family

columns.name: Order
columns.description: Taxonomic order

columns.name: Class
columns.description: Taxonomic class

columns.name: BM
columns.description: Body mass in grams

columns.name: DF
columns.description: Dominant frequency (or equivalent measure) in Hz

columns.name: Reference_BM
columns.description: Reference for body mass data
value_domain: Dunning Jr, J.B. (1992). (Ed) CRC handbook of avian body masses
Dunning, J.B. (2007). CRC handbook of avian body masses. 2nd edition. CRC Press.
Encyclopedia of Life. Available from http://eol.org.
Garbutt, N. Mammals of Madagascar. 1999. Pica Press. Sussex.

Jones, K., J. Bielby, M. Cardillo, S. A. Fritz, J. O'Dell, C. D. L. Orme, K. Safi et al. 2009. PanTHERIA: a species-level database of life history, ecology, and geography of extant and recently extinct mammals. Ecology 90:2648

Lislevand, T., Figuerola, J., & Székely, T. (2007). AVIAN BODY SIZES IN RELATION TO FECUNDITY, MATING SYSTEM, DISPLAY BEHAVIOR, AND RESOURCE SHARING: Ecological Archives E088‐096. Ecology, 88(6), 1605-1605.

Moen, D. S. (2019). What determines the distinct morphology of species with a particular ecology? The roles of many-to-one mapping and trade-offs in the evolution of frog ecomorphology and performance. The American Naturalist, 194(4), E81-E95.
Santini, L., BENÍTEZ‐LÓPEZ, A., Ficetola, G. F., & Huijbregts, M. A. (2018). Length–mass allometries in amphibians. Integrative zoology, 13(1), 36-45.

Smith, F.A., S.K. Lyons, S.K.M. Ernest, K.E. Jones, D.M. Kaufman, T. Dayan, P.A. Marquet, J.H. Brown and J.P. Haskell. 2003. Body mass of late Quaternary mammals. Ecology 84:3402 (updated version obtained from senior author).

Wilman, H., Belmaker, J., Simpson, J., De La Rosa, C., Rivadeneira, M. M., & Jetz, W. (2014). EltonTraits 1.0: Species‐level foraging attributes of the world's birds and mammals: Ecological Archives E095‐178. Ecology, 95(7), 2027-2027.

columns.name: Reference_DF
columns.description: Reference for dominant frequency data

columns.name: DF_comment
columns.description: Comment associated to dominant frequency data
value_domain: Mean for snort, barks and growls (most common during breeding)
Mid-point range, sister species Hystric cristata
Mid-point range. Sister species Tupaia belangeri
Sister species Miniopterus griveaudi with frequency of maximum energy
Songs, middle between range of peak frequencyes (250 - 475 Hz)
The call (bark) features a packet of intense energy in the 0-3 kHz range
squeak of a male approaching females

columns.name: shell.number
columns.description: unique ID assigned to each shell for tracking purposes

columns.name: treatment
columns.description: Unpainted shells were labeled as shell.trossulus or shell.californianus, depending on species. Painted shells were labeled as shell.trossulus.painted or shell.californianus.painted

columns.name: G
columns.description: rate of abiotic dissolution (umol/hr/g)

columns.name: OmegaAragonite
columns.description: Aragonite saturation calculated with seacarb in R

columns.name: duration
columns.description: length of incubation time (hours)

columns.name: shell.wt
columns.description: weight of shell (g)

columns.name: shell.length
columns.description: length of shell (mm)

columns.name: Sno
columns.description: Serial number for each Site.

columns.name: Site
columns.description: Village where the plot was located. There are four sites: Kibber, Kiamo, Tashigang, Thinam; each at a different elevation.

columns.name: Helper_ID
columns.description: The column is a copy of the Site column with numbers to coding and analysis ease.

columns.name: Field_no
columns.description: Field identifier within the site.

columns.name: Plot_no
columns.description: Plot identifier.

columns.name: Crop
columns.description: Crop species. There are three crops: green pea, black pea, or barley.

columns.name: Water_treatment
columns.description: There were three water treatments S1, S2, S3 with three replicates each. S1 corresponds to business as usual irrigation scheme as advised by the farmers. S2 refers to reduced irrigation and S3 corresponds to drought like conditions with least irrigation scheme for the crops. However, our water treatments are not used as a predictor variable since there were more rains in the summer season. The treatments were used as replicates and averaged.

columns.name: Irrigation_times
columns.description: Denotes the number of times the fields were watered throughout the growing season. The irrigation times and days were advised by the farmers. This ranges from 2 times for Black pea in Kiamo to 9 times for green pea in many sites depending on the water treatment as noted below.

Empty cells in this list mean that the compound in question was not tested under that particular condition.

columns.name: Chemical
columns.description: Digitized list of chemical compounds used to train the neural network. Repellency values for these compounds under different conditions in Aedes aegypti and Anopheles stephensi were collected from published documents (King, 1947, 1954; USDA, 1967).

Once a breakthrough has occurred, all subsequent timepoints are also indicated by a 1. 1 = Yes, 0 = No

columns.name: idt
columns.description: Participant ID code concatenated with sample timepoint and clinical/study visits. The instances listed as "other" indicate any clinical/study visit that is not a vaccination or a sample timepoint. Any time a participatant interacted with the study team or reported a clinical visit, that was not a COVID-19 vaccination or sample timepoint for the study, is listed as "other". For example, "other" is inclusive of things like the study's informed consent call, receipt of the influenza vaccine, and receipt of monoclonal antibodies.

columns.name: usa_or_hhsregion
columns.description: Specifies the geographic area for the estimate; either national (“USA”), or HHS regions 1 – 10.al subgroup

columns.name: week_ending
columns.description: The date of the last day (Saturday) of the week (or 2-week period) for which an estimate is made

columns.name: variant
columns.description: The name of the variant that the estimate is for. (Note that some variants are aggregates of all that variant’s subvariants.)

columns.name: share
columns.description: The proportion of the variant

columns.name: share_hi
columns.description: The upper bound of the 95% confidence interval for the estimated variant share (prediction intervals for smoothed estimates).

columns.name: share_lo
columns.description: The lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for the estimated variant share (prediction intervals for smoothed estimates).

columns.name: nchs_or_count_flag
columns.description: True if weighted estimate is based on fewer than 10 sequences or any of the following items is identified as unreliable by NCHS standards: degrees of freedom, effective sample size, denominator count, absolute confidence interval width, or relative confidence interval width (https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data/series/sr_02/sr02_175.pdf)

columns.name: modeltype
columns.description: Estimate is based on weighted data ("weighted') or Nowcasted data ("smoothed")

columns.name: time_interval
columns.description: Whether data is base on one week interval ("weekly") or two week intervals ("biweekly")

columns.name: lEPG
columns.description: log(number of parasites per gram of feces + 1)

columns.name: Population
columns.description: The population (lake) from which the data were collected

columns.name: eDNA
columns.description: The average concentration of eDNA observed in the study lake, derived from 4 samples collected in the littoral zone and 4 samples collected in the pelagic zone, with the final mean weighted by the proportion surface area represented by each zone in the study lake

columns.name: N
columns.description: The number of fish per hectare inhabiting each lake, estimated through mark-recapture.

columns.name: copy.per.fish
columns.description: The number of copies of eDNA per L of water per fish/ha

columns.name: Mean.Mass
columns.description: The average size of fish present in each lake, estimated from index-netting

columns.name: Biomass
columns.description: The total biomass/ha of fish in each lake

columns.name: copy.per.gram
columns.description: The number of copies of eDNA per L of water per gram of fish/ha

columns.name: Population
columns.description: The study lake from which the individual was captured.

columns.name: Mass
columns.description: The body mass (in grams) of the captured individual.

columns.name: Lake
columns.description: The lake/population from which the data are derived

columns.name: Station
columns.description: The station from which the eDNA estimate was obtained

columns.name: eDNA
columns.description: The concentration of eDNA (Copies/L) from the station sampled

columns.name: density.ha
columns.description: The density of fish harvested from each lake (i.e., fish/ha harvested)

columns.name: mean.mass.g
columns.description: The average body mass (in grams) of the fish harvested from the lake

columns.name: cluster
columns.description: Cluster name: name of site grouping containing 2-7 plots in similar landscape

columns.name: site.name
columns.description: Site name: Name for site containing one 0.1 ha plot

columns.name: year
columns.description: Year of sampling (If spring and fall sampling occurred in different year, this is year of fall sampling)

columns.name: burns.since.2000
columns.description: Fire frequency: Number of prescribed fires conducted between 2000 and the year of sampling

columns.name: pct.sand
columns.description: Percent soil sand content: Mean of 40 soil cores (4 taken every 5 meters along transect)

columns.name: pct.water
columns.description: Percent soil water holding capacity: Proportion of oven dried weight to saturated wet weight, pooled site-level sample

columns.name: soilPC1.inv
columns.description: Larger values = higher organic matter, silt and clay content, and water holding capacity: Soils principle component axis (Includes soil organic matter, soil texture (percent sand, silt, and clay content), Mehlich-III phosphorus, Bray-II phosphorus, pH, total ion exchange capacity, and water holding capacity. The first PC axis described 60% of variation in soil variables and was positively related to percent silt, percent clay, soil organic matter, total exchange capacity, and water holding capacity and negatively related to percent sand.)

columns.name: mean.dens.cor
columns.description: Canopy openness: Mean of 40 densiometer readings (4 taken every 5 meters along transect)(Proportion of 96 cells not covered by canopy X 1.04)

columns.name: mean.litdp
columns.description: Leaf litter depth: Mean of 40 depth measurements (4 taken every 5 meters along transect)

columns.name: thinning.canopy
columns.description: Canopy thinning intensity: total DBH of cut stumps/(total DBH of cut stumps + total DBH of standing trees)(Trees = 5 + cm DBH)

columns.name: thinning.shrub
columns.description: Shrub thinning intesnity: # cut shrub and sapling stems/(# cut shrub and sapling stems + # live shrub and sapling stems)

columns.name: mean.plotSR
columns.description: Average quadrat groundlayer species richness: Mean of SR in 10, 1X1 m quadrats

columns.name: bigplotSR
columns.description: Total plot species richness: All species observed in 0.1 ha plot

columns.name: Reproduction
columns.description: Whether the individual reached reproduction (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Collared
columns.description: Whether the individual was collared for tracking (Yes, No).

columns.name: Human_density
columns.description: Density of humans in the individual's natal territory in number of inhabitants per km2.

columns.name: Inbreeding
columns.description: Inbreeding coefficient of the individual based on the pedigree.

columns.name: Average_gravel_road
columns.description: Average gravel road density in the natal territory in km per km2.

columns.name: Country
columns.description: Birth country of the individual: location of the centroid of the natal territory (S = Sweden, N = Norway)

columns.name: Wolf_density
columns.description: Number of bordering neighbouring territories, i.e. the number of territories overlapping with the natal territory.

columns.name: Dist_non_breeding_zone
columns.description: Distance in m between the centroid of the natal territory and the nearest non-breeding zone, i.e. area where the wolves were not allowed to establish.

columns.name: Sex
columns.description: Sex of the individual (M = Male, F = Female).

columns.name: Snow_depth
columns.description: Average snow depth in cm in the natal territory during cold month: from the 1st of May (average birth of pups) to 30th of April next (standardized).

columns.name: Moose_density
columns.description: Hunting bag records of moose counted as killed/10 km2 for counties. Data on hunting bag records was generated as a weighted average of the moose density of the counties overlapping with the natal territory.

columns.name: Date_of_death
columns.description: Date of death for the individual, if known; otherwise recorded as "null". Dates are stored in numeric format, with origin set to "1899-12-30".

columns.name: Legally_killed
columns.description: Whether the individual was legally killed (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Birth_year
columns.description: Year of birth of the individual.

columns.name: Parental_pair_id
columns.description: Identifier for the parental pair.

columns.name: Age_known
columns.description: Whether the age of the individual is known with certainty or estimated (1 = Yes, 0 = No).
